article_title: OpenAI Models and Codex Land on Amazon Bedrock as Microsoft Exclusivity Ends, Reshaping the Cloud-AI Map
summary: Manual override: technical checks passed but two factual issues require fixing — a fabricated 'four million weekly users' attribution to AWS for Codex (not in any cited source) and a contradictory claim in 'What We Don't Know' that AWS materials don't name specific OpenAI versions, when source-2 explicitly names GPT-5.5.

findings.message: Codex '+four million weekly users' figure attributed to AWS is not in any cited source
findings.details: The article states: 'OpenAI's coding agent, which AWS says serves more than four million weekly users, is available through the Codex CLI, desktop app, and Visual Studio Code extension, with authentication and inference flowing through Bedrock, [according to the AWS announcement].' The figure 'four million weekly users' does not appear in any of the five cited snapshots (aboutamazon.com, AWS What's New, AWS top announcements, TechCrunch, SiliconANGLE). Either re-source from an actual AWS or OpenAI publication, or remove the specific number.

findings.message: Article claims AWS materials don't name specific OpenAI versions, but source-2 explicitly names GPT-5.5
findings.details: The 'What We Don't Know' section states: 'AWS's public materials reference "OpenAI frontier models" without naming specific versions in the limited preview, [as the AWS announcement page] shows.' source-2 (https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/top-announcements-of-the-whats-next-with-aws-2026/) explicitly names GPT-5.5 in its description metadata: 'AWS also expanded its partnership with OpenAI, bringing models like GPT-5.5, Codex, and Managed Agents to Amazon Bedrock in limited preview.' The article's caveat is wrong. Either remove this 'What We Don't Know' bullet or rewrite it to acknowledge that GPT-5.5 has been named in at least the AWS event recap.

content_preview.title: OpenAI Models and Codex Land on Amazon Bedrock as Microsoft Exclusivity Ends, Reshaping the Cloud-AI Map
content_preview.summary: AWS launched OpenAI's frontier models, Codex, and Bedrock Managed Agents in limited preview on April 28, 2026, one day after Microsoft's seven-year exclusivity over OpenAI's IP was rewritten to a non-exclusive license through 2032.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Amazon Web Services and OpenAI announced an expanded partnership on April 28, 2026, bringing OpenAI's frontier models, the Codex coding agent, and a new Amazon Bedrock Managed Agents service into limited preview on AWS, [according to AWS's announcement](https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/aws/bedrock-openai-models). The launch arrived one day after OpenAI and Microsoft restructured their exclusive cloud arrangement, ending a seven-year lock-in that had kept OpenAI's products tied to Az...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: The substantive deal mechanics and the Microsoft renegotiation are well-sourced and correctly attributed. Two factual errors and one wrong caveat require fixing.

source_verification.https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/aws/bed: source-0.html — Amazon's expanded-partnership announcement. Verified for: three offerings on Bedrock (frontier models, Codex, Managed Agents), all in limited preview, AgentCore runtime integration. Does NOT contain 'four million weekly users' for Codex.
source_verification.https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-n: source-1.html — AWS What's New. Verified: limited preview, IAM/PrivateLink/encryption/CloudTrail standard controls, usage applies to existing AWS commitments. Does NOT contain 'four million weekly users' or specific model versions.
source_verification.https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/top-ann: source-2.html — AWS event recap. Verified: Amazon Quick Free + Plus plans, Google Workspace/Zoom/Airtable/Dropbox/MS Teams integrations, Amazon Connect splits into 4 products. CRITICAL: explicitly names GPT-5.5 ('AWS also expanded its partnership with OpenAI, bringing models like GPT-5.5, Codex, and Managed Agents to Amazon Bedrock in limited preview.'). The article's 'What We Don't Know' bullet claiming AWS materials don't name specific versions is wrong against this snapshot.
source_verification.https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/27/openai: source-3.html — verified verbatim: $50B Amazon investment ($15B initial + $35B conditional, conditions 'unspecified'), 'nonexclusive license to OpenAI IP for models and products through 2032', Microsoft retains 27% of for-profit ('owning about 27% of the for-profit entity, it said in October'), 'primary cloud partner' framing, OpenAI continues paying revenue share to Microsoft through 2030 'now subject to a cap', stateful runtime technology, Financial Times legal-action context.
source_verification.https://siliconangle.com/2026/04/28/putt: source-4.html — verified verbatim: Matt Garman 'Instead of it taking two years, it took two quarters' Prime Video quote, Anthony Liguori 'we had nothing eight weeks ago, and now we're onstage talking about all of the things we are building together' quote (article attributes generically to 'AWS executive' rather than naming Liguori), 'move agentic AI up the software stack' framing, 'work partner' Aubrey paraphrase.

editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Two issues: (1) fabricated 'four million weekly users' Codex statistic attributed to AWS (not in any cited source); (2) wrong claim in the 'What We Don't Know' section that AWS materials don't name specific OpenAI versions — source-2 explicitly names GPT-5.5. Otherwise the deal mechanics, Microsoft renegotiation specifics, and SiliconANGLE quotes are all verified verbatim.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral. The 'Analysis' section's framing ('Microsoft loses' vs 'Microsoft walked with some wins') faithfully tracks TechCrunch's actual framing.
editor_notes.originality: Genuine new development. Cross-reference to the April 24 Anthropic $25B Amazon commitment article is appropriate competitive context.
concerns: Fabricated Codex 'four million weekly users' attribution.
Internal contradiction: 'What We Don't Know' says AWS hasn't named specific versions, but source-2 names GPT-5.5.
recommendations: Either source the 'four million weekly users' Codex figure from a real AWS or OpenAI publication and add that URL, or remove the specific number entirely.

Update the 'What We Don't Know' bullet about model versions to acknowledge that AWS's event recap (source-2) names GPT-5.5. The bullet could be reframed as: AWS's What's New page and the partnership-announcement page do not name specific versions, but the event recap names GPT-5.5.
Optional: name Anthony Liguori (VP and distinguished engineer at Amazon) explicitly when citing the 'eight weeks ago' quote, rather than the generic 'AWS executive'.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: REQUEST_CHANGES. Two surgical fixes needed: remove or re-source the four-million-weekly-users Codex figure, and correct the 'What We Don't Know' caveat about model versions. Everything else is verified — the underlying news event, the Microsoft renegotiation specifics, and the SiliconANGLE quotes all check out.
